steps.text: Start with $b - 65 = \frac{x}{y}$. To solve for $x$, multiply both sides by $y$: $y(b - 65) = x$.
Expand the left side: $y \cdot b - 65 \cdot y = by - 65y$. So $x = by - 65y$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $x = \frac{b-65}{y}$ - divides instead of multiplies by $y$. Eliminated.
  • B: $x = by - 65$ - multiplied $b$ by $y$ but not 65. Eliminated.
  • C: $x = \frac{by - 65}{y}$ - incorrectly divides the entire expression by $y$ again. Eliminated.
  • D: $x = by - 65y$ - correctly distributes $y$ across both terms. That confirms D is correct.
